Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Simple The ArcaneDoor campaign targets transportation systems using a simple yet effective attack chain involving spear-phishing, command execution, and data manipulation.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Web server services such as emails and databases, Authentication issues

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2020-29583 [CWE-22]: Microsoft Office memory corruption vulnerability
stage.description: The ArcaneDoor campaign initiates the attack by sending spear-phishing emails with malicious attachments exploiting a vulnerability in Microsoft Office.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2024-7593 [CWE-94]: Unrestricted file upload vulnerability in web application
stage.description: Upon successful phishing, the attackers execute commands remotely via a vulnerable web application, allowing them to gain control over the target system.

stage.name: Impact
stage.surface: Web server services such as emails and databases
stage.description: With access to the target's web servers, the attackers manipulate data, potentially leading to unauthorized access, modification, or deletion of sensitive information.

Scenario.summary: The ArcaneDoor campaign employs a simple yet effective attack chain targeting transportation systems. Starting with spear-phishing, they gain initial access, execute commands remotely, and ultimately manipulate data within the target's web servers.
